name: P21
subtitle: P021 | CNTF-Derived Neurogenic Peptide
overview: P21 is a nootropic peptide derived from ciliary neurotrophic factor (CNTF) that enhances neurogenesis and cognitive function. It contains an adamantane moiety that enables blood-brain barrier penetration. Research shows it increases BDNF expression, inhibits LIF signaling to promote neurogenesis, and reduces tau and amyloid pathology in Alzheimer's disease models. Remarkably, P21 can boost neurogenesis in diseased brains above levels seen in healthy untreated brains.
mechanism: P21 operates through multiple pathways: it inhibits leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF) signaling, removing a key roadblock to neurogenesis and shifting the brain toward a more embryologic state favoring neuron growth. It increases brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) expression and activates the BDNF/TrkB/PI3-K/AKT/GSK3β pathway. This pathway modulation improves cognitive function and reduces tau and amyloid pathologies.
keyBenefits: Potent promotion of neurogenesis in the dentate gyrus
Restored synaptic deficits in cortex and hippocampus
Reduced mortality rate in AD models

items.name: Alzheimer's Disease
items.description: P021 markedly reduced tau pathology, attenuated Aβ generation, and rescued episodic memory impairment in 3xTg-AD mice.

items.name: Neurodegeneration Prevention
items.description: Treatment during synaptic compensation period can prevent neurodegeneration and reduce mortality.

items.name: Tau Pathology
items.description: Robust attenuation of tau pathologies through BDNF/TrkB/PI3-K/AKT/GSK3β pathway.

items.name: Neurogenesis
items.description: Enhances dentate gyrus neurogenesis so effectively it exceeds levels in healthy untreated brains.

items.name: Memory Processes
items.description: Enhances memory processes through increased BDNF and restored synaptic function.

items.name: Age-Related Cognitive Decline
items.description: May reduce natural decline in learning and memory in aged models by rescuing neurogenesis deficit.

items.name: Synaptic Plasticity
items.description: Restores synaptic deficits in cortex and hippocampus.

deliveryMethods.overview: P21 has not yet been tested in human clinical trials. Research protocols in animal models use subcutaneous or intraperitoneal injection. The adamantane moiety enables blood-brain barrier penetration for CNS effects.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly

interactions.notes: Different nootropic mechanisms; may complement each other.
Both promote neurogenesis through different pathways.
No known negative interactions; different mechanisms.

references.title: P021 Treatment in 3xTg-AD Mice
references.keyFindings: P021 markedly reduced tau pathology, attenuated Aβ, rescued episodic memory, and reduced mortality in AD mice.

references.title: P21 and BDNF/TrkB Pathway
references.keyFindings: Disease-modifying effect via BDNF/TrkB/PI3-K/AKT/GSK3β pathway; rescued neurogenesis and cognition.

references.title: P21 in Cognitive Aging
references.keyFindings: May reduce age-related cognitive decline by restoring BDNF expression and synaptic function.

references.title: P21 as Promising AD Drug Candidate
references.keyFindings: Beneficial effects in preclinical studies make P21 highly promising for AD drug development.

quickStats.typicalDose: Research compound - dosing not established for humans
quickStats.cycleDuration: Not established - experimental peptide without human trials
